title: Oplonia minor
intro: Oplonia minor, also known as Small Water Wryneck or Water Wryneck, is a tropical aquatic plant species native to the Old World tropics. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Oplonia minor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin infections.

Its leaves are said to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. Some studies suggest that Oplonia minor may also have antioxidant effects, which could contribute to its overall health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Oplonia minor.
